page: Microsoft Azure E48ads_v6 is an Eadv6-family virtual machine configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, VNNI, and BF16, and cryptography standards like AES and SHA_NI. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

The instance is equipped with 2835 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. The memory configuration provides 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, placing it in the memory-optimized category. In benchmark evaluations, the memory subsystem demonstrated strong performance in cached read-only small block bandwidth, ranking in the top 25%. However, uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth tests yielded average results, placing in the middle 50% tier.

Compute performance is robust, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance reaching the top 10% tier, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores rank in the top 25%. The server excels in database operations, with Passmark database tests and Redis SET operations scoring in the top 25%. For machine learning workloads, the CPU architecture delivers top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models, though performance drops to average or weak tiers when processing larger 70B parameter models due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48ads_v6 offers a high density of memory and local storage relative to its compute resources, making it a viable alternative to GPU-accelerated instances for memory-heavy workloads that do not require specialized accelerators. It is highly suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation pipelines, and medium-scale data processing tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E48ads_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Eadv6 family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, this x86_64 server includes 24 physical cores and is bundled with 2835 GB of local storage. It delivers strong performance across various benchmarks, with single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 10% and multi-core capabilities in the top 25%. The system is highly capable in database operations, showing strong results in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests. While it lacks dedicated GPU hardware, its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage make it highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications. The E48ads_v6 is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, software compilation, and enterprise workloads requiring significant memory capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ads_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ads_v6 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-capacity local storage and dedicated AMD EPYC processors for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor at 3.7 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
2835 GB of local NVMe SSD storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
Optimized for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads
